title: Termination, Budgets, Timeouts, and Progress Detection · Independent Harness Trial
summary: Stop an agent safely under success, failure, budget, and no-progress conditions. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a loop termination and budget policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Terminate only when completion evidence satisfies the goal contract.
Classify retryable, recoverable, authority-blocked, and terminal failures.
Budget turns, tokens, time, tool calls, money, and external side effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Graceful Handoff condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Graceful Handoff applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Stop with a resumable state, evidence, and explicit unmet conditions.
